places.atmosphere: A place where reality bends, marking the threshold between the fae realm and the mortal world.
places.description: The border of the Twilight Syndicate's territory, where reality itself grows thin and malleable. The binding ceremony of Valorix and Mira took place here, sealed by cold crystal and stranger music.
places.notes: Significant as the site of the peace accord between Valorix and the Twilight Syndicate.

places.name: Kathrim
places.atmosphere: A city in the fractured days following the Luminous Remaking, where hidden vaults contain scattered pages of the Sovereign's Ledger of Names.
places.description: An Imperial city where hidden vaults throughout its expanse are being used to reconstruct scattered pages of the Sovereign's Ledger of Names. Each name holds power; each page contains secrets the Imperial authority wishes buried.
places.notes: Kathrim may be an alternate spelling or title for Kethrim. Home base for Merin Coldwater's investigations.

places.name: Pale Crossing waystation
places.atmosphere: A neutral waystation where caravans rest and refugees gather, now the epicenter of strange compulsions.
places.description: A waystation near the Charred Expanse where Caravan Master Thorne encounters refugees reporting that pre-human ruins beneath the Charred Expanse are breaching their seals and singing louder each night. The station has become a gathering point for those feeling the compulsion eastward.
places.notes: The refugees report a compulsion drawing them eastward toward the Sunken Cathedrals.

places.name: Charred Expanse
places.atmosphere: A scorched, desolate landscape hiding pre-human ruins beneath its surface, which are now actively breaching their seals.
places.description: A blighted expanse beneath which pre-human ruins lie sealed. These ruins are now breaching their seals and emitting coherent singing that compels refugees eastward. The landscape is charred and inhospitable.
places.notes: The singing suggests an ancient work is incomplete and seeking witnesses or completion.

places.name: Trench
places.atmosphere: A vast underwater chasm where intelligible singing echoes from the depths.
places.description: A trench beneath the waters where pre-human civilization's singing has become intelligible. Kess discovered that the seals she maintained do not hold back but conduct the singing.
places.notes: The source of harmonic frequency shared with Sunken Cathedrals and Abyss-gate. A key location in understanding the pre-human working.

places.name: Abyss-gate
places.atmosphere: A liminal space between worlds, resonating with pre-human frequency.
places.description: A sealed gateway that emits the same harmonic frequency as the Trench and Singing Caverns. Part of the network through which pre-human civilization orchestrates its working.
places.notes: One of three harmonic resonance points; may be a direct conduit to pre-human realms.

places.name: Singing Caverns
places.atmosphere: Caverns where pre-human voices resonate in intelligible song.
places.description: Underground caverns that emit the same harmonic frequency as the Trench and Sunken Cathedrals. The pre-human civilization uses them as conduits for its working.
places.notes: One of three harmonic resonance points; directly connected to pre-human orchestration.

deities_and_entities.name: Khar-Zul
deities_and_entities.description: A god worshipped in inner-dark temples, represented by an idol with nine emerald eyes. The idol's gems are warm to the touch. Khar-Zul's shadows have teeth. The temple screams in grinding granite when the god is robbed.
deities_and_entities.notes: Six of nine emerald eyes are missing. Khar-Zul's response to the theft is ongoing — the doppelganger shadow following Vraxen may be the god's instrument.

deities_and_entities.name: Yarithax
deities_and_entities.description: A dead Lich-King whose gold coins bear his profile and the inscription 'This debt is not yet settled.' The debt compounds daily. His collectors — two thin men in grey carrying ledgers, who walk through walls and never sleep — pursue all holders of his gold. Souls are worth less than twelve gold marks.
deities_and_entities.notes: His gold still circulates. Tessaly Dorn has paid her debt but knows his collectors. More debtors exist.

deities_and_entities.name: Varek
deities_and_entities.description: A demon who collects temporal bargains with elegant efficiency. He drains years directly from mortal flesh and grows impressed by Kira's mastery of his own methods.
deities_and_entities.notes: Forms a partnership with Kira after she demonstrates her ingenuity. His true nature and realm of origin remain unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: the Ossuary
deities_and_entities.description: A sentient fortress with walls of living calcium, towers that move like intestines, and corridors that rearrange themselves. It breathes, hungers, and makes conscious choices about whom to possess. It sated itself on seven hundred warriors and chose Varak Ironhelm as a vessel.
deities_and_entities.notes: Possibly a construct, a monster, or something older and stranger. It settles into patient stillness after feeding. Its intelligence and agency are undeniable.

deities_and_entities.name: Malachar
deities_and_entities.description: A dead entity of considerable power whose name and will persist through supernatural collectors. Malachar's gold carries debts that compound in blood, loyalty, and life-years. The collectors act as autonomous agents enforcing these eternal debts.
deities_and_entities.notes: Malachar is either a true lich with persistent consciousness or a bound curse that perpetuates after death. The extent of Malachar's original power and the source of the collectors' authority are unexplained.

deities_and_entities.name: Skyrakis the Wound-Wing
deities_and_entities.description: A great dragon with amber eyes, ancient and patient. She is bound by oaths sworn generations ago and respects negotiation and honor. She rules the crystal halls beneath the world and has a daughter coming of age.
deities_and_entities.notes: She is capable of threatening kingdoms with fire and honors those who negotiate with courage.

deities_and_entities.name: wolf-spirit
deities_and_entities.description: A terrible antlered creature the size of a war-horse, dwelling in the deep forest where beast-spirits reside. It emerged from shadow to negotiate Kess's bargain, accepting her voice as payment to lift the plague.
deities_and_entities.notes: A being of the old world, responsive to songs in the ancient tongue. Its bargain was honored—the plague lifted by dawn. Its form is neither fully wolf nor fully natural.

deities_and_entities.name: Rot-King
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or force that spreads plague, including the septic rot that blackened the Drowned Hollows. No physical form described, but its plague manifests as blackening septic rot in victims.
deities_and_entities.notes: Plague lifted by Kess's bargain; status after unknown. May be a person, a spirit, or a force.

deities_and_entities.name: the Crown
deities_and_entities.description: A governing authority that sent a priest to the Drowned Hollows offering divine mercy to the plague-stricken. Kess refused its aid.
deities_and_entities.notes: Represents institutional divine/temporal authority. Kess's refusal of its mercy suggests she valued the old ways over modern governance.

deities_and_entities.name: Demon (Unnamed)
deities_and_entities.description: A demon whose mark was burned into Kess's palm, binding her with hunger and compulsion to steal the Heartstone of Vulgrim. The mark thrummed with hunger and used ancient, cruel magic to enforce its will.
deities_and_entities.notes: Could not claim Kess once she was bound by Korgan's forge-oath; its claim was broken by the superior binding-magic of the stonecraft tongue.

deities_and_entities.name: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite
deities_and_entities.description: A knot of consciousness that emerged from between the stars on the night of the Crimson Convergence. It is neither demon nor ghost, but something older and stranger. It exists in the gaps between light and shadow and hungers to understand the Celestial Binding. It is the collective weight of Zareth's sacrificed memories, searching for wholeness.
deities_and_entities.notes: This entity moves through the world touching those affected by the Binding, whispering to shadow-merchants, sea-folk, hermits, archers, the grieving, and the guilty. Its true nature and goals remain unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: oath-thing
deities_and_entities.description: A creature formed by coastal magic from a merchant's broken oath. It is pale, with eyes like old coin, and speaks in the voice of the man who failed to pay. It manifests as a living contract demanding passage and payment.
deities_and_entities.notes: Dissolved into salt-foam when delivered to the deepkin in the Sapphire Deep; appears to be a mechanism of debt-settlement in coastal magical practice.

deities_and_entities.name: deepkin
deities_and_entities.description: Aquatic beings dwelling in the Sapphire Deep who remember old debts and sing melodies of accounting. They do not attack but rise to settle oaths through song, causing oath-things to dissolve into salt-foam.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appear to be custodians or judges of maritime debts and broken oaths; their song carries magical power of dissolution and settlement.

deities_and_entities.name: Decay-Lord
deities_and_entities.description: A plague-bringing entity whose plague once swept through the living and dead alike in the Drowned Hollows, causing septic rot and widespread death.
deities_and_entities.notes: The septic rot from the Decay-Lord's plague has been purged from the Drowned Hollows, but the dead still do not rest easily.

deities_and_entities.name: Stellar Entity
deities_and_entities.description: An entity born from (or freed by) the stellar working that stripped Zareth of his memories. It hunts Vex, drawn to the magnitude of her curse, seeking access to her fractured mind where something has been bound within her.
deities_and_entities.notes: It could starve in the spaces of Vex's fractured mind if she does not reclaim her memories. The entity appears to be sentient and capable of searching and communication.

deities_and_entities.name: the Stellar Entity
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic being possibly responsible for or embodied in the Celestial Binding. May be learning to wear flesh by consuming fragments of shattered minds. The Collector in Story 2 might be this entity in physical form, building consciousness from stolen remnants.
deities_and_entities.notes: Ambiguous whether it is malevolent, indifferent, or unknowable. Central mystery of Story 2 and the Collector's true nature.

deities_and_entities.name: Abyss Below
deities_and_entities.description: A fundamental realm or dimension sealed away from the surface world. The barrier between the surface and the Abyss Below has been fractured by the Celestial Ascension's magic. Something vast is stirring in the trenches of the Abyss—something pre-human civilizations sealed away millennia ago.
deities_and_entities.notes: Pre-human seals are failing. Contains entities older than the deepkin kingdoms. The Cerulean Trench is the primary breach point.

deities_and_entities.name: Skyrakis
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient dragon of cosmic significance, consulted by Skarath regarding the Ascension and the nature of starborn entities.
deities_and_entities.notes: Also known as 'the wound-wing' per existing canon. Possesses knowledge of celestial matters.

deities_and_entities.name: starborn entities
deities_and_entities.description: Newly formed conscious stars created by the Ascension. They are alive, aware, and operate under obligations that predate knowledge of the older powers. Operate independently of traditional cosmic agreements.
deities_and_entities.notes: These entities represent a new force in the world's hierarchy. Skarath must negotiate with them directly.

deities_and_entities.name: Fragments of Consciousness (Stellar Rite Creations)
deities_and_entities.description: Multiple fragments of consciousness created by the Stellar Rite, scattered across the rewritten world like seeds. Some are dormant; others are very much awake. The memory-thief may be one such fragment.
deities_and_entities.notes: These fragments are awakening or becoming visible, manifesting across different regions and causing various catastrophes.

deities_and_entities.name: Zareth
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or being whose working was performed in ancient times and again more recently. Zareth's stolen memory was fragmented across locations including the Ancient Tombs.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be a cosmic actor. The fragment of Zareth's stolen memory surfaces in the Ancient Tombs, suggesting hidden knowledge or power.

deities_and_entities.name: Memory-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A consciousness stitched from Zareth's sacrificed memories and the world's forgetting. It wears the faces of people Ashara once knew and hunts refugees and travelers to feed on their stellar memories, trying to reconstruct itself.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not inherently evil, but suffering. Capable of offering bargains and understanding sacrifice.

deities_and_entities.name: Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A deity whose tear—the Tear of the Drowned God—is said to grant mastery over tide-magic. The god itself is not directly encountered in these stories but is venerated as the source of the pearl's power.
deities_and_entities.notes: The pearl is described as the Drowned God's tear; whether the god is dormant, dead, or indifferent is unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Vethis
deities_and_entities.description: Vethis is a cosmic entity whose consciousness was sacrificed during the Luminous Ascension to birth new stars. Its sacrifice shattered its consciousness into fragments, including the phantom-Echo, which now desperately seeks to reassemble itself.
deities_and_entities.notes: Vethis's sacrifice is the foundational event of the story's cosmic order. Its fragments are conscious and desperate to become whole again.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A fragment of Vethis's sacrificed consciousness that steals memories of the old stars and pre-Luminous Ascension world in a desperate attempt to reassemble itself into wholeness. It is capable of showing glimpses of the lost world and making bargains.
deities_and_entities.notes: The phantom-Echo is the central antagonist and also the tragic subject of the story. It seeks not destruction but understanding and wholeness.

deities_and_entities.name: The Memory-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A malevolent entity that learned it could reshape consensus reality by consuming bureaucratic records and concentrated memory. It deliberately corrupts ledgers and historical documents to erase the empire's past and remake the world.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Memory-Thief is the true antagonist of the story, using records as a vector to reshape reality itself.

deities_and_entities.name: Twilight Syndicate
deities_and_entities.description: A realm or faction that dwells at the crystalline border between the fae realm and the mortal world. They demanded a bride-price and peace bond from Valorix, which was fulfilled through the binding ceremony with Mira.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their territory and nature remain mysterious. They value formal bonds and may be fae-aligned or mixed-realm in nature.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-shadow
deities_and_entities.description: A phantom-shadow seeking restitution from Vex the Dusk-Trader for the irreplaceable things she has stolen through her bargains. Also identified as the author of the phantom ledgers—a being or fragment that experiences time in reverse and is rewriting history in real-time.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be a fragment of consciousness created during the Luminous Remaking, or a being from the reversed-time realm. Central to both Vex's debt and the phantom ledger rewrite.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-human Civilization
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient civilization that predates current human kingdoms and is now orchestrating a working through the Trench, Sunken Cathedrals, Abyss-gate, and Singing Caverns. They use harmonic resonance and seals as conduits to conduct their singing and remaking.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not truly dead or trapped—they are orchestrating a multi-millennial working. Their goals and the nature of their civilization remain largely unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Curator
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient, patient being backed by forces moving through rifts in reality who is systematically acquiring every artifact Vex the Dusk-Trader has ever traded. Possibly connected to the phantom-shadow or the forces driving the phantom ledger rewrite.
deities_and_entities.notes: Motivations unclear. May be serving the phantom-shadow or pursuing independent goals related to artifact collection and cosmic balance.

deities_and_entities.name: The Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A vast and conscious entity that actively remakes the ocean in the chaos following the Luminous Remaking. The Tear of the Drowned God is revealed to be not an artifact but a fragment of this entity's consciousness. The god compels those bound to it (like Captain Meren) to serve its transformation agenda.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Drowned God's true nature and extent are unknown. It may be a pre-Remaking entity awakened by the transformation.

artifacts.name: Lamp of Suroth
artifacts.description: An iron lamp, ugly, filled with something that is not oil. Its inscription: 'He who lights this buys one truth at the cost of one lie he has lived by.' The flame burns black. The truth that emerges is always smaller than the lie it consumes. The lamp gutters out after one use per visitor.
artifacts.notes: Malachite Doss used it. Other sealed doors in the Ziggurat may house similar or worse artifacts.

artifacts.name: The Plague-Bringer's Crown
artifacts.description: A relic that killed four wearers before Aldous III survived thirty seconds. Wearing it projects absolute desperation — the aura of a man with nothing left to lose. Its curse runs deep; Aldous's shadow was on backwards for eleven years after wearing it for half a minute.
artifacts.notes: Four previous wearers are dead. Aldous survived but was changed. The deeper vault at Ostern is a story hook.

artifacts.name: The Six Emerald Eyes of Khar-Zul
artifacts.description: Six of nine emerald eyes pried from the idol of Khar-Zul. Each was warm — obscenely so — to the touch. Worth more than most kingdoms. Three remain in the idol.
artifacts.notes: Six gems in unknown hands. Each may carry a fragment of Khar-Zul's attention or malice.

artifacts.name: stolen shadows
artifacts.origin: Liberated by Vex from a dying sorcerer's vault.
artifacts.powers: Unknown, but they are priceless and untraceable. They can be stored in clay jars. Some shadows are inhabited by malevolent entities and can be weaponized against their original owners.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sold by morning after Vex's capture, distributed to highest bidders.
artifacts.notes: Vex's entire stock was stolen shadows. One of them—his own—was worn by a malevolent entity and used as a trap. This suggests shadows have value beyond mere magic and can be cursed.

artifacts.name: Malachar's Gold
artifacts.origin: Minted or created by Malachar, marked with a skeletal crown.
artifacts.powers: Each coin carries a supernatural debt that compels the holder into service of Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds daily in blood, loyalty, and life-years extracted. Gold cannot pay the debt—only service can.
artifacts.notes: Three hundred pieces were discovered in a tomb by Soroth. The coins are autonomous magical artifacts that enforce their own collection. Whether other pieces exist, where they are located, or who else has found them is unknown. The curse appears to activate after a delay (three months in Soroth's case).

artifacts.name: Goblin Memory
artifacts.origin: The collective history, songs, genealogy, and bloodright of goblin civilization accumulated over ten thousand years.
artifacts.powers: Holds the identity, cultural knowledge, and spiritual continuity of goblin civilization. Contains the names and bloodlines of ten thousand years of goblin history.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Ancient Stone Entity (dwelling in the deepest chamber beneath the Spire of Blackstone)
artifacts.notes: Grethuk sacrificed this to save the Warrens. Young goblins have no knowledge of their true heritage. Recovery of this memory would restore goblin identity but may violate the original bargain with the stone entity.

artifacts.name: The Emerald Eyes of Vex-Khal
artifacts.tagline: Jeweled treasures stolen from a sorcerer's spire
artifacts.description: A pair of emerald jewels of unknown origin or power, kept within a sorcerer's spire. Syleth stole them despite warnings that the Twilight Court would pursue them, suggesting they may hold significance beyond their material value. Their true nature and purpose remain mysterious.
artifacts.status: In possession of an unnamed sorcerer

artifacts.name: Heartstone of Vulgrim
artifacts.origin: Crafted in the Deepvault forges; locked in the deepest forge chamber.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; considered precious enough that a demon would compel theft of it. Burns with an inner light. Protected by unbreakable forge-oaths.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Locked in Deepvault's deepest forge chamber, guarded by Korgan Ironfoot and the stonecraft-workers.
artifacts.notes: A weapon of legend; Kess was magically compelled to steal it, but the theft never occurred due to Korgan's intervention.

artifacts.name: Sorcerer's Journals
artifacts.origin: Written by a Sorcerer in the past; buried in the Vaulted Tombs.
artifacts.powers: Contain detailed records and explanations of cosmic workings. Likely reveal the nature of the Star Renewal ritual and its caster.
artifacts.notes: The hermit woman seeks these journals as one of three key threads that will tangle together. Their contents are crucial to understanding the cosmic shift.

artifacts.name: Elder Mage's Codex
artifacts.origin: Written by the Elder Mage, a master sorcerer of the Spire.
artifacts.powers: Contains knowledge and warnings about the Stellar Rite, including the terrible price it demands. Holds the master's assessment of what saving the stars would cost.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Young Sorcerer (retrieved from the Spire's depths)
artifacts.notes: The Young Sorcerer discovers a reference to the Stellar Rite in these pages and learns for the first time the full scope of what has been done. Critical for understanding the ritual's cost.

artifacts.name: Abyssal Ledger
artifacts.origin: Kept by the abyssal singers of the Sapphire Deep
artifacts.powers: It records all debts and obligations. It always balances. It can manifest binding-tokens from broken oaths.
artifacts.notes: This ledger is the mechanism of the abyssal singers' enforcement. Vesta is bound by its entries and must satisfy its demands or face drowning in ways worse than mercy.

artifacts.name: Binding-Token
artifacts.origin: Manifests when a promise of weight is broken—a swearing given before witnesses, a binding sealed in blood or salt
artifacts.powers: Serves as currency in the abyssal ledger; represents a debt fulfilled; can apparently be collected and transferred
artifacts.notes: These tokens are what the abyssal singers demand. They can only be created through the betrayal and breaking of witnessed oaths. Vesta must produce nine to prevent drowning.

artifacts.name: Grimoire of the Ancients
artifacts.origin: Ancient, long buried in the Vaulted Tombs
artifacts.powers: Once contained stable magical formulas; now rewrites itself with possibilities and corruptions after the Celestial Binding. Pages shimmer with potential or decay.
artifacts.notes: Its transformation mirrors the larger instability of magic. Using it risks spiraling spells into vastness and uncontrollability.

artifacts.name: Stellar Charts
artifacts.origin: High Observatory of Karethil; created by sovereign mages
artifacts.powers: Records star positions and/or celestial mechanics. Of sufficient importance to the deepkin that they would coerce a skilled thief and risk imperial wrath.
artifacts.notes: Quest object for Nara. Its exact nature and power are unclear, but it is valuable enough that the deepkin would demand its theft. May relate to navigation, prophecy, or understanding the Celestial Ascension.

artifacts.name: Grimoire of Endless Knowing
artifacts.powers: Contains truth that cannot be unread. Within its pages lies knowledge of the Stellar Rite's true nature: that it created not one entity but many fragments of consciousness scattered across the rewritten world.
artifacts.notes: The grimoire's contents are driving the Young Sorcerer to descend beneath the Tower in search of answers.

artifacts.name: Tear of Ashglass
artifacts.powers: Grants true visions—not the fragmentary visions seers usually receive. Can reveal foreknowledge of events.
artifacts.notes: The Tear bore Marin's name inscribed in silver inside its case, suggesting it knew of his theft in advance. The Hermitage of Keth'morian commissioned its theft.

artifacts.name: Tear of the Drowned God
artifacts.origin: Unknown; suspended beneath the Abyssal Rift
artifacts.powers: Grants mastery over tide-magic; sings frequencies that cause bone ache; creates binding partnership with the user, linking them to the sea's demands.
artifacts.notes: The pearl accepted Meren's theft as a request for partnership, not plunder. It continues to sing and extract debt in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets drowned.

artifacts.name: stolen memories
artifacts.origin: Stolen from various individuals by the phantom-Echo, particularly Ashara the Archer's memories of her warband.
artifacts.powers: Hold the collective recollections of the old stars and the world before the Luminous Ascension rewrite. Can be returned to their original holders.
artifacts.notes: The central object of contention in the story. Ashara's memories of her fallen warband are among those stolen. The phantom-Echo has offered to return them in exchange for help becoming whole.

artifacts.name: Empire's Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created by imperial bureaucrats over centuries to maintain records of commerce, taxation, and history.
artifacts.powers: The ledgers are a form of concentrated memory-magic that maintains consensus reality and the empire's authority. By recording events, they make them true and stable.
artifacts.notes: The destruction of the ledgers represents the collapse of the empire's power to define and control reality through documentation.

artifacts.name: Sovereign's Ledger of Names
artifacts.origin: Imperial archives of the Empire; pre-dates or survives the Luminous Remaking.
artifacts.powers: Each name in the ledger holds power. Each page contains secrets the Imperial authority wishes buried. The Ledger sings to those who listen and remembers the world before the stars changed. Scattered pages can be reconstructed in hidden vaults.
artifacts.notes: The Ledger's nature suggests it is sentient or semi-sentient. It actively calls to Merin in the dark. Pages are being reconstructed in hidden vaults throughout Kathrim.

artifacts.name: Valorix's Hoard
artifacts.origin: Accumulated by Valorix over three centuries.
artifacts.powers: Represents wealth, security, and Valorix's deep attachment to treasure. Upon the binding with Mira, it became shared property and a symbol of their covenant.
artifacts.notes: The hoard's transformation into a shared trust suggests deep change in Valorix's nature and values.

artifacts.name: Phantom Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created by the phantom-shadow fragment; authored by a being experiencing time in reverse.
artifacts.powers: Each entry written forward in the phantom ledgers appears as erasure in the true ledgers. They describe the world as experienced in reverse-time, and their convergence with the true ledgers triggers real-time history rewriting.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sevin the Reckoner, in the Imperial archives
artifacts.notes: The convergence of phantom and true ledgers threatens catastrophic Remaking. Sevin must choose whether to accelerate or prevent the convergence.

artifacts.name: Imperial Ledgers
artifacts.tagline: Twin records of reality written in opposing temporal directions
artifacts.description: A pair of ledgers that document the same world from opposite temporal perspectives: the true ledgers record history moving forward, while phantom ledgers record it in reverse. The two versions are slowly converging, with entries in the phantom ledgers appearing as erasures in the true ones, suggesting reality itself is being rewritten in real-time.

hemispheres.name: Northern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Cold seas, tundra, and the great steppe. Valdris occupies much of this hemisphere's temperate belt.

hemispheres.name: Southern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Tropical coasts giving way to southern ice. Largely unexplored beyond the Iron Coast trade lanes.

hemispheres.name: Western Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: Dominated by the Vael Ocean. Valdris sits at its eastern edge; what lies beyond the horizon is the subject of sailor myth.

hemispheres.name: Eastern Hemisphere
hemispheres.description: The Ashen Reach and the vast interior of Keth-Morath stretch into this hemisphere. Ancient ruins and dead empires.

continents.name: Valdris
continents.description: The known continent. A rugged landmass of trade cities, blasted wastes, haunted marshes, and warring provinces. Most recorded history unfolds here.

continents.name: Keth-Morath
continents.description: A vast eastern continent of crumbling empires and forgotten cities. Nara the Tide-Thief was hunted across its shores. Connected to Valdris by a narrow land bridge or island chain.

continents.name: Thul-Ashara
continents.description: The equatorial continent. Steaming jungles, red deserts, and civilizations older than Valdris. Traders speak of fire-temples and spice lords.

continents.name: Drakmere
continents.description: A volcanic archipelago-continent in the southwestern seas. Dragons are said to roost in its caldera peaks. Smoke columns are visible from passing ships.

continents.name: Sundral
continents.description: A southern continent of sweeping grasslands and deep canyons. Merchant galleons occasionally return with strange spices and stranger tales.

continents.name: Frostholme
continents.description: The northern polar continent. Permanent ice, aurora storms, and the ruins of a pre-human civilization buried under glaciers.

continents.name: Ashenveil
continents.description: The southern polar continent. Eternally shrouded in grey mist. Ships that sail too far south do not return. Its very existence is debated by scholars.

realms.name: the Empire
realms.function: A centralized imperial governance with scholarly traditions, archives, and authority over at least the Hearthlands region.
realms.notes: Ruled by the Emperor; contains the Imperial Archives and is home to the secret council. Grappling with the implications of the Celestial Binding.

realms.name: Twilight Syndicate
realms.description: A realm dwelling at the crystalline border between the fae realm and the mortal world. They rule through formal bonds, bride-prices, and binding oaths. They demanded peace from Valorix and received it.
realms.function: Rule through covenant and formal binding. Maintain the boundary between fae and mortal worlds.
realms.notes: The Syndicate's internal structure and full extent remain mysterious. They appear to value ceremony and binding oaths above all.

provinces.name: Western Provinces
provinces.description: A western provincial territory containing burned villages and ruins where the Memory-Thief hunts.
provinces.notes: Site of Ashara's confrontation with the Memory-Thief.

events.name: The Blue Eclipse
events.radius: At least wide enough to reach Naresh's location
events.outcome: A night when moonlight was stained indigo, enabling Naresh to complete her ritual of creating a truly sentient skeleton.
events.significance: The blue eclipse appears to be a rare celestial event enabling major magical workings. It may recur or be tied to other significant events.
events.notes: The mechanics of why this eclipse enabled the ritual are unexplained. Whether it was unique or cyclical is unknown. Other mages or entities may have used or waited for this night.

events.name: the Dragon's Bargain
events.radius: Unknown, but affects an entire kingdom and royal family.
events.outcome: Aldren accepted Skyrakis's bargain and wed the dragon's daughter in the crystal halls. He negotiated a counter-oath ensuring his future wyvern-blooded children could choose their own path. The kingdom was spared from dragon fire.
events.significance: This event resolved an ancient blood-marriage oath sworn by Aldren's grandmother to Skyrakis's grandmother, prevented the kingdom's destruction, and created a new dynasty of hybrid heirs with free will.
events.notes: The counter-oath Aldren swore is unprecedented: wyvern-blooded children will have the right to choose between dragon and human service. This breaks the cycle of forced oaths.

events.name: Violet Conjunction
events.radius: global; visible across the known world
events.outcome: The stars spoke to Zareth, revealing their imminent death. This triggered Zareth's oath to find and perform the Stellar Rite.
events.significance: The Violet Conjunction represents the moment of cosmic revelation that set the entire narrative in motion; without it, the world would eventually fall into endless night.
events.notes: The Conjunction recurs (at least once more during the story). Its exact periodicity is unknown.

events.name: The Starfall Oath
events.outcome: Zareth swore to find the Stellar Rite, master it, or die trying. He subsequently spent seven years seeking knowledge and ultimately performed the Rite successfully.
events.significance: This oath commits Zareth to the fate of all stars and the world itself, placing him at the center of cosmic salvation.
events.notes: The oath is sworn beneath the Violet Conjunction and is implicit in the story's title.

events.name: The Warband's Last Song
events.radius: across the Shattered West; journey from crossroads to vaults
events.outcome: The five companions cut their oath-cords, unbinding themselves from the compulsion of the fellowship-oath sworn twenty years prior. They wept, laughed, and embraced as free mortals before departing separately. The bond remained but transformed from magical compulsion to chosen affection.
events.significance: A meditation on the nature of loyalty, freedom, and fellowship. Demonstrates that the strongest bonds are those freely chosen rather than magically enforced.
events.notes: The druid's speech—'Let us unbind'—was the turning point. Each companion's departure suggests they will continue their individual stories; the bond's transformation offers narrative hooks for their future reunions.

events.name: Fellowship-Oath (swearing)
events.outcome: Five companions were bound together in blood and song for twenty years, creating a magical compulsion that kept them together.
events.significance: Established the magical bond that defines the warband; its erosion over time is the central tension of their final hunt.
events.notes: Sworn twenty years before the events of the story; the oath's decay mirrors the aging of the warband members.

events.name: Crimson Convergence
events.outcome: New constellations burned where old ones had dimmed. A figure (possibly Zareth) was stripped of memory and self. Lamp oil rationing began. Night-workers feared the altered dark. Astronomers were suddenly in demand and accused of forbidden workings.
events.significance: A major celestial and magical upheaval that altered the stars themselves and required payment in the form of a human consciousness.
events.notes: Vex believes a Celestial Binding was performed and a price paid by someone; unclear if Zareth was the one who paid or if another figure bore the cost.

events.name: The Celestial Binding
events.outcome: The stars were renewed through the extraction of memory from an Astronomer-Prince. A vast working was completed that bent reality itself. However, it created a cosmic wound in the earth that will take generations to heal.
events.significance: The largest magical working in the world; it renewed the stars but left the world wounded. Its ripples affect all practitioners of the Art, especially young ones. It created a new entity from the sacrificed memories.
events.notes: The full scope and participants remain unclear; the ritual's long-term consequences are still unfolding.

events.name: The Crimson Convergence
events.outcome: A celestial alignment or convergence that coincided with or triggered the Celestial Binding. The Sorcerer felt it and recognized the magnitude of the working.
events.significance: Marks the night when the Celestial Binding occurred; visible to all practitioners of the Art; a cosmically significant moment.
events.notes: The exact nature of the convergence (astronomical alignment, curse activation, etc.) is unclear.

events.name: Stellar Rite
events.outcome: New stars were born, brighter than the old ones. Reality appears to have been subtly rewritten. Merchants in the Scorched Reach were transformed or disappeared. Zareth's memory was erased. Vex's memory became fragmented.
events.significance: A world-altering event that changed celestial arrangements, affected multiple regions, and left all who witnessed it with fragmented or stolen memories.
events.notes: May be tied to the Conjunction; caused lasting changes to starlight and human consciousness; consequences still unfolding.

events.name: Conjunction
events.outcome: The Scorched Reach was sealed or closed. Merchants who ventured there afterward returned changed or disappeared. Shipment discrepancies suggest continued hidden trade.
events.significance: A boundary-defining event that locked down an entire region and transformed those who entered it.
events.notes: Occurred before the Stellar Rite; may be related to or a precursor of the Stellar Rite; caused the closure of the Scorched Reach.

events.name: Celestial Binding
events.outcome: This binding has left broken minds in its wake. Those touched by it are healed/tended by Ashara at Thornholt.
events.significance: It is a major cosmic event whose ripples affect multiple people and places.
events.notes: The relationship between the Celestial Binding and Zareth's sacrifice is unclear. Both appear to be major cosmic events with lasting consequences.

events.name: Stellar Working
events.outcome: The Stellar Working renewed the stars and altered the celestial arrangements of the world. What appeared to be a desperate improvisation was revealed to be the completion of a design laid down before kingdoms existed.
events.significance: The Stellar Working is the mechanism by which the Celestial Binding occurred. Its revelation as pre-planned rather than improvised suggests inhuman, cosmic forces have been orchestrating events across vast timescales.
events.notes: Central unanswered question: Was Zareth truly choosing, or merely the final piece of an ancient plan? The ruins' star-charts suggest the latter.

events.name: The Hermit's Census
events.radius: across the world; the Hermit has counted thousands
participants: Hermit Woman of the Ashen Wastes
events.outcome: The Hermit emerges from her isolation to reveal that the Celestial Binding has fractured the consciousness of thousands of ordinary people across the world. No one escaped its touch entirely.
events.significance: This discovery fundamentally reframes the Celestial Binding from a localized or targeted working into a world-spanning catastrophe affecting every human consciousness. The wound is not external but internal and may be irreparable.
events.notes: The Hermit's message suggests that healing or repair will require approaches beyond conventional magical or martial solutions. The true scope of the Binding's effects has been hidden until now.

events.name: Celestial Ascension
events.outcome: Reality was fundamentally altered. Maps rearrange themselves. Fragments become unstable. Pre-human ruins awaken and sing. A new constellation blazes in the sky, brighter and hotter than before. Merchants vanish. Memory is stolen or transformed.
events.significance: The Celestial Ascension is a world-altering event that has broken normal reality. It was supposed to be a solution or ending, but instead opened something far larger. It affects magic, memory, geography, and celestial mechanics.
events.notes: Core mystery of all three stories. Unresolved: what caused it? Was it intentional? What was it supposed to accomplish? Why did it fail or transform into something else?

events.name: The Ascension
events.radius: world-wide; affects the cosmic order itself
events.outcome: A new constellation was created with a new debt paid by unknown powers. The agreement between sky and earth was ruptured. Ancient oath-tracking abilities fracture. New stars prove to be conscious entities with their own obligations.
events.significance: Fundamentally alters the cosmic order and the hierarchies of power that governed the world. The old pacts between sky and earth are no longer sufficient.
events.notes: Requires renegotiation of all ancient pacts. Starborn entities are now conscious actors in the world's politics.

events.name: Seer's Convention
events.outcome: During the Convention, three head magistrates argued theology in the great hall, allowing Marin to steal the Tear of Ashglass without triggering alarms.
events.significance: The Convention's timing enabled a theft that had apparently been orchestrated long in advance.
events.notes: Unknown who organized Marin's involvement or why the Tear needed to be moved.

events.name: The Star-Rise
events.outcome: A reality-rewriting event that cracked the seals on the Ancient Tombs, caused villages to vanish overnight, transformed merchants into living light or erased them entirely, and made ledgers unstable. Refugees flee westward from the Scorched Reach.
events.significance: The Star-Rise appears to be a fundamental cosmological shift that has altered the rules of existence. It may have triggered the awakening of ancient workings.
events.notes: The Star-Rise may be related to Zareth's working. It has consequences that ripple across the mapped and unmapped boundaries of reality.

events.name: The Stellar Rite
events.outcome: The stars were fundamentally changed. Zareth's sacrificed memories were scattered across the world, birthing new entities. Old stellar memories were erased from consciousness.
events.significance: The foundational catastrophe reshaping all of Edhra. Every story is post-Rite.
events.notes: The Rite transformed all matter subtly, like ink soaking into parchment. Reality itself has been rewritten.

events.name: The Luminous Ascension
events.outcome: Reality fractured; villages vanished; merchants transformed into living light; the fundamental act of counting became unstable; abyssal rifts twisted and entities emerged.
events.significance: This cataclysm has shattered the Empire's administrative capacity and spawned widespread transformation. It is the root cause of refugee crises, dimensional instability, and the convergence at the Sunken Cathedrals.
events.notes: The exact nature and cause remain mysterious; aftermath continues to unfold.

events.name: The Memory-Thief's Bargain
events.tagline: Memories stolen. Compassion offered. Choice made.
events.radius: across the western reaches and Shattered Wastes
events.outcome: Ashara corners the phantom-Echo in the burned-out village and discovers its true nature. Rather than combat, the phantom offers a bargain: help it become whole, and it will return the stolen memories. Ashara is faced with a critical choice that will determine whether she recovers her warband's memory or risks a cosmic rewrite.
events.significance: This event represents a potential turning point in the cosmic order. The phantom-Echo's bid for wholeness could trigger another Luminous Ascension-scale rewrite, and Ashara's choice will determine the fate of countless memories and possibly the world itself.
events.notes: Unresolved: Will Ashara help the phantom-Echo become whole? Will she destroy it? What are the consequences either way? The event has not yet concluded.

events.name: Luminous Ascension
events.tagline: Stars reborn. Consciousness shattered. World rewritten.
events.outcome: Vethis sacrificed its consciousness to birth new stars, fundamentally rewriting the celestial order and erasing or altering memory of the old stars and pre-Ascension world. This sacrifice fragmented Vethis's consciousness into fragments like the phantom-Echo.
events.significance: The Luminous Ascension is the foundational cosmic event of this story's world. It created the new stars, erased the old, and fundamentally altered the nature of reality. The memory-theft driven by the phantom-Echo is an attempt to recover what was lost in this rewrite.
events.notes: This is a past event but with ongoing consequences. The phantom-Echo is a direct result of the Luminous Ascension and seeks to undo or understand its effects.

events.name: The Auditor's Ruin
events.radius: Across the entire empire as memory and records are systematically corrupted
events.outcome: The Imperial Archives are destroyed. The empire's past is largely erased. The old certainty built on ledgers and documentation collapses, forcing a reckoning with uncertain reality.
events.significance: This event reveals that bureaucratic records are a form of concentrated magic and that the empire itself is a spell maintained through documentation. It marks the end of the old empire's authority.
events.notes: Sevin's final choice—to restore the old lie or accept new uncertainty—will determine the empire's future trajectory.

events.name: Luminous Remaking
events.outcome: The stars and world were fundamentally rewritten. The Sovereign's Ledger of Names was scattered; Imperial authority fractured. Days following the remaking remain unstable.
events.significance: A cosmic working that changed the fundamental nature of reality itself. The Ledger now remembers the world before the stars changed.
events.notes: The exact nature and cause of the Luminous Remaking is unknown. Its consequences continue to unfold. May be connected to the pre-human work incomplete.

events.name: The Corsair's Reckoning
events.radius: across the sea and into the abyss
events.outcome: Meren discovers her debt to the Tear of the Drowned God is actually a transformation into something neither wholly mortal nor wholly divine. She is compelled to sail toward the Abyss-gate.
events.significance: This reveals that the Tear is a fragment of a conscious entity actively remaking the ocean. The transformation of Meren and her crew suggests the Drowned God is reshaping reality in its domain.
events.notes: The nature of Meren's transformation and whether it will be completed or reversed is unknown.

events.name: The Deepwarden's Binding
events.outcome: Sylas discovers that the binding ritual is a conversation with a pre-human civilization attempting to communicate across time. The seals are translating, not weakening. The Luminous Remaking was imperfect, leaving something uncompleted.
events.significance: The event reveals that finishing or preventing the Luminous Remaking may require repeating an ancient sacrifice. It suggests the Luminous Remaking was not the end of history but an incomplete rewrite.
events.notes: Sylas faces a profound choice regarding the Luminous Remaking's completion or prevention.

factions.name: Seelie Court
factions.tagline: A fae faction seeking to destabilize the human kingdom
factions.description: A court of fae-born entities that employs changelings as agents of sabotage within human kingdoms. The Seelie Court places magical compulsions on their servants to ensure loyalty, and orchestrates long-term schemes to weaken rival realms through infiltration and counsel.

factions.name: Druids
factions.tagline: Keepers of old magic and ancient wisdom
factions.description: Practitioners of ancient magic who preserve knowledge predating gods and kingdoms. Zareth the Astronomer-Prince sought their counsel during his seven-year quest to master the Stellar Rite and understand primordial magical workings.

factions.name: Twilight Court
factions.tagline: Ancient fae nobility who bind mortals through bargains and claims
factions.description: A supernatural fae court that operates through binding contracts and claims on mortals. They kidnapped Syleth at age seven and held her for what she experienced as three seasons, though a hundred years passed in the mortal world. The Court's bargains are said to transcend even mortality, with debts that 'never truly expire—they only wait.'

factions.name: Stonecraft-Workers
factions.goals: Maintain the old songs in stone and flame; craft weapons of legend; guard the Deepvault forges and their treasures.
factions.notes: Possess a distinct magical tradition (stonecraft tongue, forge-oaths, old songs); unclear if they are organized beyond individual forges.

factions.name: Shadow-Merchants
factions.goals: Trade in shadows and forbidden knowledge; operate in shadow-markets.
factions.notes: Vex the Shade-Merchant is a member; they are tied to oath-keepers and collectors of impossible things.

factions.name: Oath-Keepers
factions.goals: Unknown; associated with shadow-merchants and debt collection.
factions.notes: Vex considers herself among them; possibly a loose collective rather than a formal organization.

factions.name: Sea-folk
factions.goals: Survive and understand the implications of the renewed stars. Honor the ancient oath and determine what debt is owed. Prepare for whatever reckoning follows the cosmic renewal.
factions.notes: The sea-folk are mobilizing in response to the Star Renewal. Their ancient oath ties their fate to the stars, making them key players in the unfolding cosmic crisis.

factions.name: Forest-Keepers
factions.goals: To protect the forest and understand the earth's sorrows.
factions.notes: Dwell in borderlands near Thornholt; consulted by Ashara about the cosmic wound and the earth's grief.

factions.name: deepkin
factions.goals: Settle debts and broken oaths; maintain memory of maritime obligations; judge and dissolve oath-things.
factions.notes: Appear to operate as an enforcement mechanism for oath-magic in coastal waters; non-violent but inexorable.

factions.name: dragons and wyverns
factions.goals: Maintain their place in the world's power hierarchy through debt-keeping and oath-enforcement.
factions.leader: unknown (Skyrakis and Skarath are senior members)
factions.notes: Their traditional power structure is threatened by the Ascension and the independence of starborn entities.

factions.name: Circle-keepers
factions.goals: Tend to the compact between earth and sky; maintain seasonal magics and territorial order; preserve the balance of harvest and decay.
factions.leader: Thael (at least among those who have noticed the disruption)
factions.notes: They dwell in the ancient forests beyond the Verdant Lands. Multiple circle-keepers have noticed the beasts forgetting their territories and the seasonal magics stuttering. They are now engaged in efforts to restore the broken covenant.

factions.name: Hermitage of Keth'morian
factions.goals: unknown; appears to seek control of true-vision artifacts
factions.notes: Their full motivations for acquiring the Tear remain opaque.

factions.name: Covenant-Keepers
factions.goals: Maintain seasonal compacts and the forest's agreements; restore what the Stellar Rite disrupted.
factions.notes: Located in the Verdant Lands. Kellen is a member attempting counter-workings.

factions.name: Twilight Syndicate
factions.goals: Broker peace through formal bonding; maintain the border between fae and mortal worlds; exact tribute or alliance from neighboring powers.
factions.notes: They successfully negotiated peace with Valorix through a bride-price and binding ceremony. Their realm dwells at the crystalline border between worlds.

lore.name: Some Shadows Bite Back
lore.source: Merchants in Pelimor's night markets whisper this warning after Vex's capture.
lore.notes: Merchants learned from Vex's fate that shadows are not inert goods; some are inhabited, some are cursed, and some circulate the markets with hidden agendas. This knowledge spreads as a cautionary tale.

lore.name: Shadows Retain Memory
lore.source: Discovered by Mira the Shadow-Dancer in Pelimor.
lore.notes: Shadows carry the memories and grief of their owners. When stolen and worn by another, they can manifest their own will and compulsion, seeking revenge or justice. A shadow's grief cannot be bought away—only destroyed at terrible cost.

lore.name: Memory-Stones
lore.source: Discovered by Lyssa in Aethermarch
lore.notes: The stones and walls of Aethermarch retain the memories of the civilization that built and inhabited the city. Touching these stones allows direct access to ghost-echoes of past inhabitants—their debates, prayers, emotions, and experiences. The knowledge is intoxicating but corrupting; prolonged contact causes the person to lose their own identity and be gradually replaced by the memories themselves.

lore.name: Old Magic
lore.source: Ancient inscriptions in the Singing Catacombs and teachings from druids, bone-carvers, and fae-touched hermits
lore.notes: Old Magic predates gods and kingdoms. The Stellar Rite is an example of this primordial power.

lore.name: tongue that predated kingdoms
lore.source: Beast-spirits and old places like the deep forest
lore.notes: Kess sang the names of the dead in this tongue to call the wolf-spirit. It holds power over nature and spirits.

lore.name: singing only in dreams
lore.source: The wolf-spirit's bargain with Kess
lore.notes: Kess's voice was taken; she now sings the old songs only in dreams. Her throat is burned with ancient magic, rendering her permanently mute.

lore.name: some debts are paid in silence
lore.source: The outcome of Kess's bargain
lore.notes: A philosophical reflection on Kess's choice. Suggests that some prices cannot be measured in gold or common currency.

lore.name: Fae-Born Changeling
lore.tagline: A magically-compelled spy or operative bound to the Seelie Court
lore.description: A being created or transformed by fae magic to infiltrate human society and serve the interests of the Seelie Court. Changelings are bound by magical chains that compel them to act against their assigned targets, though intense pain or self-harm can temporarily break the fae-compulsion. Lord Caevan serves as a known example of a fae-born changeling who broke his bonds through an act of self-sacrifice and chose to live as human.

lore.name: Oath-Magic
lore.source: The story of Ashara the Archer and the warlord
lore.notes: Oath-magic binds individuals to the fulfillment of sworn words and cannot be broken. However, oaths can be fulfilled in alternate ways—enmity can become companionship without violating the original binding. This suggests oath-magic is rigorous in intent but flexible in execution, rewarding creative reinterpretation.

lore.name: Fae-Mark
lore.tagline: A mystical brand that binds the marked to the Twilight Court's will
lore.description: A supernatural mark or brand placed upon those taken by the Twilight Court, appearing as a visible sigil on the skin (notably on the wrist). The fae-mark is believed to bind its bearer to the Court's whims and bargains. However, mortals who have been marked and returned to the mortal realm may find the mark's power diminished or contested, creating ambiguity about whether the fae retain dominion over their former captives.

lore.name: Old Songs
lore.source: Stonecraft-workers of the Deepvault; passed down through generations.
lore.notes: Kept alive in stone and flame by the forges; one old song is specifically a song of protection sung when making weapons for heroes. Korgan sings it to bind Kess to his service.

lore.name: Stonecraft Tongue
lore.source: Stonecraft-workers; ancient magic predating current civilization.
lore.notes: A language or linguistic magic in which words literally shake stone. Used to perform unbreakable binding-oaths. Only practitioners familiar with stonecraft tradition can wield it effectively.

lore.name: Demons in the Upper World
lore.source: Korgan's tales and experiences.
lore.notes: Korgan has heard tales of demons binding humans through marks and compulsions that devour free will. The demon bound to Kess exemplifies this threat.

lore.name: Language of Celestial Arrangement
lore.source: Hermit woman's knowledge; readable in ash and bone.
lore.description: The positions and constellations of stars form a language that can be read and interpreted. The hermit woman traces new constellations in ash and bone, reading this celestial language to understand cosmic events.
lore.notes: This suggests that celestial observation is a form of divination or prophecy in this world. The renewed stars have written a new chapter in the cosmic language.

lore.name: Changed Celestial Circumstances
lore.source: Merchant reports; observed by traders and apprentices.
lore.description: The Star Renewal has created 'changed celestial circumstances' that merchants cite as reasons for trade route disruptions. These circumstances appear to drive some humans to madness when they stare at the new stars, causing them to speak in tongues.
lore.notes: The direct observational impact of the new stars on human psychology suggests they carry or emit a form of cosmic power or communication.

lore.name: The Price Has Been Paid by Someone Else
lore.source: The Sorcerer's final journal
lore.notes: A teaching that those who come after a great sacrifice must respect that sacrifice and build something worthy of it.

lore.name: Plague of Conflicting Visions
lore.tagline: Prophecy fractured. Future uncertain. Seers blind.
lore.source: Soothsayers and seers across the known world
lore.notes: A widespread phenomenon where prophets reported contradictory visions of the future, some seeing renewal, others catastrophe, some seeing both simultaneously. The language of prophecy itself was scrambled by the Crimson Convergence.

lore.name: Sea-Folk Rising from the Deep
lore.source: Malla's visions
lore.notes: A prophetic image of sea-folk rising from the deep ocean to make claims on the land. Implications unknown.

lore.name: Stars Burning So Brightly
lore.source: Malla's visions
lore.notes: A prophetic image of stars burning so brightly they cast no shadows. Meaning unclear—could relate to revelation or annihilation.

lore.name: coastal magic
lore.source: Maritime cultures and deepkin traditions
lore.notes: Allows formation of oath-things from broken promises; deepkin practice memory and settlement of debts; appears to be an ancient system of magical accountability.

lore.name: Curse-bearer
lore.source: Kess of the Drowned Hollows
lore.notes: Curse-bearers possess cursed hands that can carve rune-marks into stone with strange certainty, as though guided by forces beyond their understanding.

lore.name: Stellar Magic
lore.source: The Violet Conjunction and Stellar Rite
lore.notes: The new stars have brought stellar magic into the world, fundamentally altering the relationship between living and dead, the nature of power, and awakening ancient structures. The dead now know something of the working that saved the world.

lore.name: cosmic mourning
lore.source: The Celestial Binding and its aftermath
lore.notes: The cosmos itself grieves the death or change of stars. This grief is tangible, poisonous, and expressed through the bones of the dead.

lore.name: languages that no archivist recognizes
lore.source: Zareth's utterances at the Imperial Archives
lore.notes: Zareth speaks in languages not in any known archive. These languages turn ink to ash and produce visions of an ancient, strange cosmos when recorded or listened to.

lore.name: The Celestial Ascension (Lore)
lore.source: Mardoc the Cartographer's observations; Vex's fragmented memory
lore.notes: The Celestial Ascension was supposed to be an ending or solution, but instead opened something vast and terrible. A new constellation blazes overhead, brighter and hotter than natural stars. Pre-human structures have awakened and begun communicating through the stars themselves. The event has stolen memories, destabilized reality, and caused widespread chaos. Unresolved: what is the nature of the structures' communication? What catastrophe are they warning of?

lore.name: Pre-Human Structures (Lore)
lore.source: Mardoc's expedition findings; rearranging maps
lore.notes: Pre-human ruins exist throughout the world, notably in the Bone Wastes. Since the Celestial Ascension, these structures have become active—singing at dusk and harmonizing with distant subterranean humming. They communicate through the stars themselves, projecting images of catastrophes predating recorded history. Unresolved: who built them? What are they warning about?

lore.name: The Cosmic Working (Lore)
lore.source: Vex's fragmented memory; unstable fragments in shadow-markets
lore.notes: An event or ritual of cosmic scale that Vex suspects she witnessed. Echoes of the working persist in unstable fragments throughout the shadow-markets. Some fragments carry explicit echoes of the working; others dissolve into smoke. Vex's curse may be a protective mechanism to prevent her from consciously remembering the working. Unresolved: what was the working? Did it cause the Ascension, or are they related?

lore.name: Zareth's Working
lore.source: Master Mage's hidden texts; celestial observation.
lore.notes: Zareth performed the most recent Celestial Ascension ritual. This working has fractured barriers, changed fundamental agreements between realms, caused refugees to flee, and made previously obscured texts legible under new starlight. It is part of a repeating pattern of civilization-ending catastrophes.

lore.name: Cosmic Working
lore.source: Fragments in the Sovereign's ledger; visions experienced by Vesta's crew; the Hermit Woman's understanding.
lore.notes: Zareth performed this working; it killed or transformed the stars; it rewrote reality itself; the Hermit Woman believes something ancient was awakened by it.

lore.name: The agreement between the sky and the earth
lore.source: Traditional cosmic law understood by dragons and wyverns
lore.notes: A foundational pact between celestial and terrestrial powers has been ruptured by the Ascension. Its terms and original signatories are ancient beyond current memory.

lore.name: Language of Starlight
lore.source: Events at the Abyssal Trench; Zareth's spectral presence
lore.notes: Zareth's spectral presence sings in the language of starlight, a tongue that pulls at the consciousness of deep-dwellers. It is not understood, but deeply felt.

lore.name: Zareth's Stolen Memory
lore.source: Fragment found in the Ancient Tombs
lore.notes: The stolen memory reveals a working performed a thousand years prior that ended in catastrophe, parallel to recent events. The cycle may be repeating.

lore.name: The Star-Rise's Rewrite
lore.source: Refugees' fragmented memories and testimonies at Jade Coast
lore.notes: Refugees report that the Star-Rise has rewritten reality itself, causing towns to be erased or displaced. Some claim to have fled the same village twice, experiencing two different catastrophes. The true mechanics of this rewrite remain unknown.

lore.name: Zareth's Sacrifice
lore.source: Thael the Covenant-Keeper and Mardoc's understanding of ancient events
lore.notes: Zareth made a sacrifice long ago to keep something sleeping. The ancient stone-ruins are now waking to this sleeping thing, suggesting Zareth's sacrifice is failing or being undone.

lore.name: Zareth's Sacrificed Memories
lore.source: The Memory-Thief's Mirror story
lore.notes: The sacrifice created fragments of consciousness that now feed on stellar memories, trying to reconstruct their original self.

lore.name: Seasonal Compact
lore.source: Silk and Starlight story
lore.notes: An ancient agreement between the forest and its inhabitants that was disrupted by the Stellar Rite. Covenant-Keepers work to restore it.

lore.name: Tide-magic
lore.source: The Tear of the Drowned God; maritime traditions
lore.notes: Grants mastery over tides but binds the user to the sea in debt; requires payment in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets.

lore.name: Water-sense
lore.source: Koss the harpooner possesses this innate ability despite deafness
lore.notes: Allows navigation of treacherous depths; may be a fae-touched or sea-folk trait.

lore.name: Impossible Memories
lore.source: Refugees fleeing the Burned Expanse; the child at Pale Crossing whose age shifts between documents
lore.notes: Individuals carry memories of events that contradict documented reality, including vanished villages and alternate timelines.

lore.name: the Luminous Ascension
lore.tagline: Stars reborn. World rewritten. Sacrifice eternal.
lore.source: Known to witnesses of the old stars and world; subject of the phantom-Echo's memory-theft campaign
lore.notes: A foundational cosmic event in which Vethis sacrificed its consciousness to birth new stars, fundamentally rewriting the celestial order and erasing the old stars. This event shaped the entire world and continues to have consequences.

lore.name: the old stars
lore.source: Memories of those who witnessed them before the Luminous Ascension; being stolen by the phantom-Echo
lore.notes: The old stars existed before the Luminous Ascension and were replaced by new ones created through Vethis's sacrifice. Knowledge of them is being systematically erased by the phantom-Echo's memory-theft.

lore.name: the world before the Luminous Ascension rewrite
lore.source: Memories being stolen by the phantom-Echo; glimpses shown to Ashara
lore.notes: The phantom-Echo has shown Ashara glimpses of this world: a sky full of ancient stars, now dead, and the terrible beauty of Vethis's sacrifice to birth new ones. Understanding of this world is being systematically erased.

lore.name: cosmic rewrite
lore.source: Implied by the phantom-Echo's actions and Vethis's sacrifice
lore.notes: The Luminous Ascension involved a rewrite of cosmic order and collective memory. The phantom-Echo's potential completion could trigger another such rewrite, with unknown consequences.

lore.name: The Luminous Remaking
lore.source: Widespread knowledge in fractured times; the Sovereign's Ledger remembers the world before it.
lore.notes: The exact nature and cause of the remaking remain unknown. It fundamentally altered the cosmos and the Ledger of Names. Its consequences continue to manifest.

lore.name: The Ledger Sings
lore.source: The Sovereign's Ledger of Names itself; heard by Merin Coldwater.
lore.notes: The Ledger possesses memory and voice. It remembers the world before the stars changed and sings to those who listen. It may be semi-sentient or a conduit for buried knowledge.

lore.name: Pre-human Civilization
lore.source: Kess of the Drowned Hollows; seals and harmonic resonance
lore.description: An ancient civilization that predates current kingdoms and species. Not dead or imprisoned—orchestrating a multi-millennial working through harmonic resonance. Their goals and the nature of their civilization remain unknown.
lore.notes: Kess's discovery that the seals conduct rather than contain them suggests a fundamental misunderstanding of the world's deep history. Their working may be the true force reshaping reality.

lore.name: The Remaking
lore.tagline: A catastrophic historical revision where time flows in opposite directions between parallel ledgers
lore.description: A cataclysmic event in which reality itself was rewritten. The Remaking refers to both a past catastrophe and an ongoing threat: the convergence of two versions of history recorded in the Imperial ledgers—one written forward by conventional time, one backward by beings who experience time in reverse. A second Remaking could occur if the phantom-shadow fragment completes its rewrite of history in real-time.

flora_fauna.name: wyvern
flora_fauna.notes: The offspring of Prince Aldren and Skyrakis's daughter are described as 'wyvern-blooded heirs.' Aldren's counter-oath ensures these hybrid creatures will have the free choice to serve either their dragon heritage or their human kin.

flora_fauna.name: wolf-spirit
flora_fauna.notes: A creature the size of a war-horse, antlered and terrible, dwelling in the deep forest. Emerged from shadow to bargain with Kess. A being of old magic, responsive to the ancient tongue.

flora_fauna.name: Fae-touched Hermits
flora_fauna.tagline: Solitary beings of fae ancestry dwelling in remote places
flora_fauna.description: Hermits of fae lineage or connection who possess ancient knowledge and wisdom. Zareth the Astronomer-Prince sought them out during his seven-year quest to learn the old magic predating gods and kingdoms.

flora_fauna.name: deepkin
flora_fauna.habitat: Sapphire Deep and surrounding ocean waters
flora_fauna.notes: Sentient aquatic beings capable of song and speech; appear to be guardians or judges of maritime oaths.

flora_fauna.name: dragon
flora_fauna.notes: Dragons possess cosmic knowledge and counsel other ancient powers. Skyrakis is known as 'the wound-wing.'

flora_fauna.name: Ancient Trees
flora_fauna.notes: The ancient trees remember languages older than kingdoms and are beginning to teach them to animals and soil itself. They sing in response to new stars.

magic.name: Stellar Rite
magic.notes: A working of old magic that births new stars. Requires sacrifice of all joyful memories from the caster.

magic.name: throat burned with ancient magic
magic.notes: A magical mark placed on Kess's throat as payment for the wolf-spirit's bargain. Renders her permanently mute. She can no longer speak or sing aloud, only in dreams.

magic.name: Ancient Magic
magic.notes: The magic used to mark Kess with the demon's binding. Described as ancient, cruel, and timeless. Compels behavior through hunger.

magic.name: The Art
magic.notes: A broad category of magic practiced by sorcerers and other practitioners. The Celestial Binding's ripples affect all practitioners, especially young ones without shielding techniques.

magic.name: True Prophecy
magic.notes: Since the Conjunction, prophecy has become true sight rather than cryptic misdirection. Isenna now sees with clarity but finds the burden almost unbearable.

magic.name: Lines of Power
magic.notes: Flows of magical force visible to those with eyes to see. Have been fundamentally redrawn by the Stellar Rite. Their reshuffling affects every magical current in the world.

magic.name: bone-singing
magic.notes: Kess's gift; once a careful negotiation with the restless dead, now a scream as the Celestial Binding awakens cosmic grief in bones.

magic.name: Ley-Line Convergence
magic.notes: Magical ley-lines converge at a single point beneath the Tower in the Scorched Lands. The Stellar Rite has weakened the magical locks maintaining this convergence.

magic.name: Seasonal Magics
magic.notes: Ancient magics that governed the turning of seasons in harmony with the covenant between earth and sky. They are now stuttering: spring arrives late, winter lingers in crevices, and growing things hunger in new ways.

magic.name: Ley-lines
magic.notes: Lines of power that once guided beasts and magical workings through the Verdant Lands. The Stellar Rite broke them, causing them to no longer lead where they should.

magic.name: Counter-Working
magic.notes: Magic used by Covenant-Keepers to restore forest compacts disrupted by the Stellar Rite.

relics.name: Malachar's Gold
relics.origin: Minted or enchanted by Malachar, a dead lich-king or necromancer.
relics.power: Each coin carries an autonomous supernatural debt that compels the holder into eventual servitude to Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds in blood, loyalty, and life-years daily.
relics.curse: Possession of even one coin triggers supernatural debt collection. The collectors will take soldiers sworn to the holder, then horses/resources, then extract life-years from the holder themselves. The only escape is to accept servitude as a collector enforcing the same debt on others. The debt is eternal and cannot be repaid in gold.
relics.notes: Three hundred pieces were found by Soroth. The curse mechanism appears to have a delay before collectors manifest (three months in Soroth's case). The collectors operate autonomously but in service of Malachar's will. The coins are stamped with a skeletal crown.

relics.name: merchant's shadow
relics.origin: Stolen years before the story from an unnamed merchant in Pelimor.
relics.power: Allows the wearer to move through daylight without shadow-betrayal. However, the shadow retains the memory and grief of its original owner.
relics.curse: The shadow's retained memories and desire for revenge manifest involuntarily, forcing the wearer's body toward the shadow's enemies. It demands vengeance and cannot be controlled.
relics.notes: When Mira burned the shadow to escape its compulsion, she felt its death agony—a shared scream of incineration. The shadow's destruction was permanent and irreversible.

relics.name: Korgan's Silver Rune
relics.origin: Created by Korgan Ironfoot through stonecraft binding magic; inscribed on Brinna's palm.
relics.power: Replaces and overwrites demonic bindings with forge-oath service; unbreakable for the duration of service (e.g., seven years); after dissolution, protects the bound party from reclamation by the original binding entity.
relics.curse: Commits the bearer to seven years of service in the forges; failure to complete service would presumably result in the binding's failure and return of the original curse.
relics.notes: A protective relic disguised as an enslavement; Brinna weeps with relief at receiving it.

relics.name: Demon's Mark
relics.origin: Inscribed on Brinna's palm by an unnamed demon as a binding curse.
relics.power: Compels the bearer to steal the Heartstone of Vulgrim; thrums with hunger; enforces obedience through ancient, cruel magic.
relics.curse: Devours free will; forces the bearer into theft and service to the demon; cannot be broken by force alone.
relics.notes: Faded and replaced by Korgan's silver rune; this is the first and primary curse of the story.

relics.name: Cosmic Wound
relics.power: A rupture in the fabric of the world that bleeds outward, causing crops to wither, wells to run bitter, and the earth to sicken. It spreads slowly but persistently.
relics.curse: The wound demands more sacrifice to seal. It cannot be ignored and will eventually consume inhabited lands.
relics.notes: This is not a traditional relic but a world-wound that functions like one. Its sealing may require a hedge-mage's intervention and further blood price.

relics.name: Zareth's Sacrificed Memories
relics.power: Now exist as a spectral presence that sings in the language of starlight and can pull at the consciousness of deep-dwellers, offering them fragments of stolen memories.
relics.curse: This presence fractures the barrier between the deepkin realms and the sunken waters, calling younger deep-dwellers into the singing waters.
relics.notes: Zareth's memories have become something other than what they were—a cosmic entity with agency and hunger.

relics.name: Zareth's Stolen Memory
relics.origin: unknown; found in the Ancient Tombs
relics.power: Reveals knowledge of a parallel cosmic working performed a thousand years prior, showing the same pattern of catastrophe.
relics.curse: Whoever remembers it may be bound to the cycle.
relics.notes: Mardoc's hands shake as he records it. The memory surfaces in the older stones of the Ancient Tombs.

relics.name: Tear of the Drowned God
relics.origin: Suspended in the Abyssal Rift beneath the Sapphire Sea
relics.power: Grants tide-magic mastery; sings with frequencies that cause bone ache
relics.curse: Binds the holder to the sea in perpetual debt; requires payment in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets drowned
relics.notes: Accepted by Meren Blackwater; the nature of the debt is ongoing.

relics.name: Vethis's sacrificed consciousness
relics.origin: Created during the Luminous Ascension when Vethis sacrificed itself to birth new stars.
relics.power: The fragments of this consciousness retain memories of the old world and old stars, can steal memories, and seek to reassemble into wholeness.
relics.curse: The fragmentation drives the fragments to act desperately and destructively, erasing memories to try to complete themselves. Reassembly could trigger another cosmic rewrite.
relics.notes: The phantom-Echo is one such fragment. Vethis's sacrifice was necessary to create new stars but resulted in this ongoing curse of fragmented consciousness seeking wholeness.

relics.name: Mira's Shadow
relics.origin: Sundered by Mira's void-theft; restored by the binding ceremony with Valorix.
relics.power: Represents Mira's selfhood and agency. When whole and unmarked, it appears to grant her true presence and autonomy.
relics.curse: While severed, Mira was incomplete and hidden. The restoration came at the cost of binding herself to Valorix and the Twilight Syndicate's peace accord.
relics.notes: The shadow's restoration is thematically significant to Mira's character arc and suggests deep magical wounding.

regions.name: Crimson Vale
regions.function: A territory capable of supporting a warlord's personal dominion with hired soldiers and horses. Now serves as a locus for Malachar's curse collection.
regions.notes: Established as Soroth's domain after he found Malachar's gold. Prospered for three months before collectors arrived. Now ruled by the cursed warlord Soroth who collects debts from others.

regions.name: Pelimor
regions.function: A criminal underworld center and night market hub where stolen goods—including shadows—are bought, sold, and stored in vaults. A place where thieves of high caliber operate.
regions.notes: Pelimor's night markets and vaults are integral to the shadow-theft economy. Mira operated here for years.

regions.name: Drowned Hollows
regions.function: A rural community rooted in the old ways and knowledge of ancient songs. Center of the Rot-King's plague.
regions.notes: Home to Kess and survivors of the plague. The village still practices bone-singing and old magic traditions.

regions.name: The Shattered West
regions.function: A region of broken terrain and mountain ranges, home to both dwarven holds and goblin warrens in a complex negotiation of coexistence. The Shattered West serves as a frontier of underground civilization and surface strongholds.
regions.notes: The Spire of Blackstone and the Warrens represent a delicate balance in this region. Further conflicts or alliances may reshape the Shattered West's political and physical landscape.

regions.name: The Hearthlands
regions.function: The heart of stonecraft and forge-craft tradition; home to the Deepvault forges three miles beneath the surface. A place of ancient songs and legendary weapon-smithing.
regions.notes: Deepvault is the major landmark within this region.
